articles.title: Las tasas se desploman en el mercado, pero el costo de los préstamos personales sube en términos reales
articles.content: A pesar de la caída de las tasas de interés generales, los préstamos personales se mantienen estables en comparación con 2022, afectando a familias endeudadas.

articles.title: Chau plazo fijo: así operan los principales bancos hoy, lunes 27 de abril
articles.content: Las entidades mantienen rendimientos moderados y el mapa de tasas sigue mostrando diferencias para quienes buscan cuidar pesos.

articles.title: Salarios y paritarias de metalúrgicos: cuánto cobrarán en mayo de 2026 tras el último acuerdo
articles.content: Los metalúrgicos cobrarán en mayo los sueldos de abril con escalas actualizadas. No es un aumento nuevo, sino la aplicación del último acuerdo vigente.

articles.title: Lanzan Kit 4.0, el programa del Gobierno para impulsar impulsa la transformación tecnológica de las pymes
articles.content: La iniciativa del Ministerio De Economía apunta a impulsar la adopción de tecnología e Industria 4.0 mediante aportes del FONPEC y un esquema de cofinanciamiento.

articles.title: El Gobierno pone un límite a los aumentos mensuales de AySA hasta agosto y baja el ritmo de las subas de tarifas
articles.content: La actualización tarifaria tendrá un máximo del 4% mensual hasta abril y del 3% desde mayo, según lo dispuesto por el ERAS tras la publicación de la Resolución 14/2026 en el Boletín Oficial.

articles.title: Trump no despeja Ormuz, pero sí el acceso de Warsh a la Fed
articles.content: Persisten las tensiones en Medio Oriente mientras avanza el recambio en la Fed y Wall Street mantiene su rally, impulsado por resultados sólidos y expectativas optimistas.

articles.title: Ni Shein ni Temu: la nueva plataforma para compras en el exterior que es furor
articles.content: El avance de apps de compras extranjeras cambió los hábitos, brindando una alternativa con beneficios inéditos y precios imbatibles.

articles.title: Sostienen que eliminar retenciones impulsaría exportaciones a u$s50.500 millones y sumaría recaudación
articles.content: Un informe proyecta que quitar los DEX elevaría la producción agroindustrial y generaría más divisas, mientras que el Estado podría recaudar más que con el esquema actual en el mediano plazo.

articles.title: Cuánto sale armar un cine en casa en 2026: opciones económicas para mejorar tu hogar
articles.content: Armar un cine en el hogar es posible y existen opciones económicas, siempre que se tenga claro qué se busca.

articles.title: La guerra en Medio Oriente también impacta en un alimento inesperado: se disparó el precio mundial del pistacho
articles.content: El conflicto entre Estados Unidos, Israel e Irán alteró el comercio global del pistacho, cuya producción se concentra en esa región. El valor internacional alcanzó máximos de ocho años y Argentina busca aprovechar la oportunidad con fuerte expansión del cultivo en dos provincias clave.

articles.title: Survey on the Access to Finance of Enterprises: lending conditions tightened
High Bar for Tech Earnings This Week: Covley
articles.content: Manning & Napier Senior Investment Analyst Kelly Covley says there will be a high bar this week for tech companies to justify their level of AI capex spending. She speaks to Bloomberg's Matt Miller on 'Open Interest.' (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Deutsche, JPMorgan Favor Energy-Linked Currencies Amid Iran War
articles.content: Investors are doubling down on bets that energy-linked currencies will keep rising as the US-Iran conflict reshapes global oil markets, with Deutsche Bank, JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Pioneer Investments urging clients to buy into the trade.

articles.title: RBC Hires Graham, Boccio as Heads of CDS and Electronic Trading
articles.content: Royal Bank of Canada is bolstering its credit trading business with senior hires, including new roles focused on credit-default swaps and electronic trading.

articles.title: Oil Rises as Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks Keep Hormuz Strait Shut
articles.content: Oil rose after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, leaving the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and prolonging the supply disruption that has roiled global markets.

articles.title: Citadel Securities Sees Markets Buoyed as Iran Risks Recede
articles.content: Stocks and bonds can rally in tandem as the Iran conflict moves closer to a resolution that avoids escalation, even as the stalemate around the Strait of Hormuz continues, according to Citadel Securities.

articles.title: Ackman’s Pershing Square IPO Expected to Raise $5 Billion
articles.content: Billionaire Bill Ackman’s IPO of his closed-end fund and his alternative asset management company is expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, the low end of the targeted fundraising range.

articles.title: Sequoia and Nvidia Back Ex-DeepMind Researcher’s New AI Startup at $5.1 Billion Value
articles.content: Former Google DeepMind researcher David Silver has raised $1.1 billion for his new company, Ineffable Intelligence, in the latest example of an artificial intelligence startup securing enormous funds out of the gate.

articles.title: The Real Rare Earth Crisis: Processing
articles.content: Critical Metals CEO Tony Sage joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the real crisis in rare earths isn’t mining, it’s processing. With the US facing a dangerous shortfall in critical minerals used for defense and AI, Sage outlines how China’s dominance, permitting delays, and limited refining capacity threaten national security, and how a global push is racing to fix it. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stock Rally Falters Near Record High as Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A record-breaking rally in stocks took a breather as oil climbed after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, with traders bracing for a raft of megacap earnings and central bank decisions.

articles.title: Hongqiao Said to Price $1.5 Billion Convertibles Near Low End
articles.content: China Hongqiao Group Ltd. priced its 10.2 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) sale of bonds that can be converted into stock near the low end of the marketed range.

articles.title: China blocks Meta’s $2bn purchase of AI group Manus
articles.content: Regulators had reviewed whether deal violated Beijing’s investment rules

articles.title: Inside China’s plans to fight in space
articles.content: From seizing satellites to striking Earth from orbit — Beijing is developing dual-use capabilities in an intensifying arms race with the US

articles.title: Are Europe and America headed for divorce?
articles.content: The US military commitment to Europe is fraying — but the two sides remain locked in an unhappy marriage for now

articles.title: Why the UAE asked Pakistan for its $3.5bn back
articles.content: Tensions between Abu Dhabi and Islamabad have burst into the open

articles.title: Shell buys Canadian shale producer ARC for $16bn
articles.content: Deal will boost oil major’s production at a time when investors have queried the company’s growth

articles.title: How ‘conflict entrepreneurs’ are inflaming US political violence
articles.content: Americans are increasingly accepting of ideologically motivated crimes

articles.title: Private equity backers raise new conflict concerns over sweetheart deals
articles.content: Investors question if some institutions rubber-stamp deals that could benefit other businesses

articles.title: United chief hits out at American Airlines for refusing merger talks
articles.content: Scott Kirby criticises rival for ‘closing the door’ on deal he believes would have created jobs

articles.title: Why flying less won’t do much to ease the oil crisis
articles.content: Crude demand weakens when not only jet fuel but other product prices rise too

articles.title: Gold Edges Lower as Attempts to Restart US-Iran Talks Falter
articles.content: Gold edged lower as efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran remained at an impasse, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.

articles.title: Banks Launch $2 Billion BASF Coatings Loan Sale for Buyout
articles.content: Banks have launched a debt sale worth over $2 billion for the auto paint and finishing division of BASF SE, amid a difficult market backdrop for the chemicals industry.

articles.title: Mideast Urea Output Slumps With Lack of Fertilizer Ships to Load
articles.content: More than half of the Middle East’s urea output may have been lost since the start of the Iran conflict, which is continuing to disrupt fertilizer flows from the region and threatening global food inflation.

articles.title: La industria de China aumentó sus ganancias empresarias a pesar de la guerra en Medio Oriente
articles.content: Los datos oficiales mostraron que las ganas industriales crecieron a su mejor ritmo en seis meses. Sin embargo, el riesgo de un impacto de la guerra sobre el entramado productivo chino sigue latente.

articles.title: Jeweler Claire’s to Shut All UK Stores After Latest Insolvency
articles.content: Claire’s Accessories has stopped trading in the UK and Ireland, in the latest blow to the British retail industry.

articles.title: Firms Swarm US Market With New Debt, Seizing Issuance Window
articles.content: Firms swarmed the US primary markets with new debt on Monday, seizing a window to lock in borrowing costs ahead of a raft of earnings and central bank decisions, even as uncertainty in the Middle East lingers.

articles.title: Italy’s Angelini Said to Explore Catalyst Pharma Acquisition
articles.content: Italian drugmaker Angelini Pharma is exploring a potential acquisition of Catalyst Pharmaceuticals Inc. as it looks at deals to expand its footprint globally, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: Wizz Air Has Enough Jet Fuel to Handle Strong Summer Bookings
articles.content: Wizz Air Holdings Plc expects to have enough jet fuel for the next month — despite industry warnings about potential shortages — as the budget airline logs stronger summer bookings than last year.

articles.title: ARC Resources Leads Canadian Energy Peers Higher on Shell Deal
articles.content: ARC Resources Ltd. shares were on track for their biggest gain since 2020 on Monday, leading peers higher, after Shell Plc agreed to acquire the Canadian energy producer in a C$22 billion ($16 billion) deal.

articles.title: Stonepeak and Bernhard to Buy Cleco From Macquarie-Led Group
articles.content: Stonepeak and Bernhard Capital Partners agreed to buy Cleco Group LLC from a consortium comprised of Macquarie Asset Management, British Columbia Investment Management Corporation and Manulife Investment Management.

articles.title: Haas Boss on F1 US Boom
articles.content: Haas F1 Team Principal Ayao Komatsu joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down Formula 1’s explosive US growth, the controversial new regulations, and how team culture is driving their success this season. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Nickel Rises to Two-Year High as Supplies From Indonesia Tighten
articles.content: Nickel rose to the highest in almost two years, as reduced mining quotas in major producer Indonesia and a global sulfur shortage tighten the supply outlook for the battery metal.

articles.title: Chemical Firm Arxada Gets Key Lender Support for Debt Extension
articles.content: Specialty chemical firm Arxada AG won the support of key creditors in its bid to delay debt repayments, as the broader industry faces stress from rising energy prices and subdued demand.

articles.title: Meta’s Chinese stumble suggests a declining tolerance for shades of grey
articles.content: Tech-related capital flows have benefited from decades of ambiguity, but AI changes the calculus

articles.title: Google staff urge chief executive to block US military AI use
articles.content: Over 560 employees sign open letter to Sundar Pichai following the Pentagon’s clash with Anthropic

articles.title: Canada launches C$25bn sovereign wealth fund to boost growth
articles.content: Mark Carney announces initiative amid trade tensions with the US

articles.title: Ackman’s Pershing Square IPO Expected to Raise $5 Billion
articles.content: The IPO for Bill Ackman's Pershing Square is expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg's Bailey Lipschultz reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Treasuries Slide as Mounting Oil Prices Offset Auction Results
articles.content: Treasuries fell amid rising oil prices even as the two US government debt auctions drew improved demand in the $31 trillion market.

articles.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Bankers Aim to Finance Venezuela Oil Revival
articles.content: A private-credit firm founded by former Credit Suisse bankers is seeking to finance Venezuela’s oil services sector, as one of the country’s largest energy conferences in decades draws investors back to Caracas.

articles.title: Grupo Mexico to Merge Power Unit With BlackRock-Backed Saavi
articles.content: Mexican mining and railroad conglomerate Grupo Mexico has struck a deal to merge its electricity generation operations with a BlackRock Inc. unit, creating one of the country’s biggest private power operators.

articles.title: Mag 7 Earnings Test $16T AI Rally | Open Interest 4/27/2026
articles.content: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." It's a make-or-break week for the rally as five of the Mag 7, worth nearly $16 trillion, prepare to test the AI trade. The Fed headlines a packed week of central bank decisions while Washington moves on a potential new chair. Plus, tensions in the Middle East as Iran floats a plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Rebecca Patterson warns investors may be underestimating geopolitical risks. Ed Ludlow breaks down a major shift in the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership. Steve Ketchum says private credit stress is creating opportunity. Tony Sage sounds the alarm on rare earth processing. And Haas F1 boss Ayao Komatsu joins us on the surging popularity of the sport. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Seaport Therapeutics Seeks $212.4 Million in US IPO
articles.content: Seaport Therapeutics Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on antidepressants and anxiety drugs, is seeking to raise $212.4 million in a US initial public offering.

articles.title: Emerging Stocks Reclaim Pre-War Peak on Hormuz Deal Hopes, AI
articles.content: Emerging-market currencies and equities climbed Monday even as the stalemate between the US and Iran on resuming peace negotiations left the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and pushed oil higher.

articles.title: Stonepeak and Bernhard Clinch $6 Billion Deal to Buy Cleco Power
articles.content: A consortium including Macquarie Group Ltd. has agreed to sell Louisiana utility Cleco Power to Stonepeak Partners LP and Bernhard Capital Partners, the companies said in a statement Monday, underscoring surging investor demand for electricity and grid assets.

articles.title: Private Credit Boom Turns Fund Finance Into $1 Trillion Market
articles.content: A boom in private credit drove the fund finance market past $1 trillion last year, as investment vehicles borrowed to manage liquidity and to bridge delayed exits, according to Moody’s Ratings.

articles.title: Descuentos Cuenta DNI: todo lo que tenés que saber para ahorrar este lunes 27 de abril
articles.content: La billetera virtual del Banco Provincia suma beneficios en supermercados, carnicerías, comercios de cercanía, marcas seleccionadas, ferias y librerías.

articles.title: Lanzan Kit 4.0, el programa del Gobierno para impulsar la transformación tecnológica de las pymes
articles.content: La iniciativa del Ministerio De Economía apunta a impulsar la adopción de tecnología e Industria 4.0 mediante aportes del FONPEC y un esquema de cofinanciamiento.

articles.title: Shell’s ARC Deal Seen as Win for Mark Carney’s Pro-Oil Pivot
articles.content: Shell Plc.’s acquisition of Canada’s ARC Resources Ltd. marks a vote of confidence in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s push to expand the nation’s hydrocarbon exports beyond the traditional US market, analysts say.

articles.title: Walmart Plans to Sell Investment-Grade Bonds on Monday
articles.content: Walmart Inc. raised $4.25 billion from a high-grade bond offering, boosting the size of the deal amid a flood of issuance that was mostly well received.

articles.title: Potential 'GRFT' ETF Outperforms S&P 500
articles.content: Bloomberg Intelligence's Athanasios Psarofagis joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ.". An ETF designed to track White House Policy has been in limbo for more than a year, as exchanges steer clear of what looks like a high-volatility, politically sensitive trade. The potential GRFT ETF is easily outperforming the S&P 500 this year. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Test Your ETF Knowledge In Our IQ Test
articles.content: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu puts Katie Greifeld, Athanasios Psarofagis, and Vilana Hajric to the test on "Bloomberg ETF IQ" in this week's edition of "IQ Test." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: ETFs Are Building Blocks in Portfolios: Grimba
articles.content: Valerie Grimba, director of global ETF strategy at RBC Capital Markets joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss how investors are navigating the proliferation of ETFs as they reach record trading volumes in 2026.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Goldman Offering Total Portfolio Solutions: Shupe
articles.content: Alyson Shupe, managing director and head of global product strategy at Goldman Sachs Asset Management joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Goldman recently closed its acquisition of Innovator Capital Management, an ETF issuer that was among the first to use options ​to determine outcomes or generate income.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Causes Alarm Ahead of King’s Visit
articles.content:
The White House will review security at major events involving President Trump — after a gunman targeted the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Mishal Husain explains the impact on King Charles' state visit. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stocks Hold Near Peak as Busy Earnings Week Starts: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks held near record highs at the start of a week that will feature a raft of megacap earnings and central bank decisions, with traders weighing conflicting signs about progress toward ending the Iran war.

articles.title: Vanguard, Amazon's Alexa Team Up For Proxy Voting
articles.content: John Galloway, global head of investor engagement at Vanguard , joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss Vanguard's announcement on Monday for a new integration with Amazon's Alexa, making it easier for investors to enroll in their retail proxy voting choice program.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US being ‘humiliated’ by Iran, says German Chancellor Friedrich Merz
articles.content: Atlanticist leader says ill-prepared war is hurting Europe’s largest economy

articles.title: Russian forces defeated in Saharan stronghold after wave of attacks
articles.content: Co-ordinated assaults forced Mali’s Moscow-backed regime to abandon key city

articles.title: Rogers Offers Buyout Packages to Staff, Seeking to Cut Costs
articles.content: Rogers Communications Inc., Canada’s largest wireless company, is making about 10,000 employees eligible for voluntary buyouts as the telecommunications industry deals with major growth and debt challenges.

articles.title: A Warsh-Led Fed Stands to Reignite the Treasuries Steepener Bet
articles.content: Wall Street strategists say that a Kevin Warsh-led Federal Reserve may jolt the $31 trillion Treasuries market out of its narrow trading ranges, urging investors to position for shorter-dated yields to eventually move lower.

articles.title: Need For 'Clean Beta' with ETFs: F/M Investments CEO
articles.content: Alex Morris, CEO & CIO of F/M Investments, joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Intel Draws $50 Billion of Orders for $6.5 Billion Bond
articles.content: Intel Corp. attracted about $50 billion of investor orders for a $6.5 billion bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, as the chipmaker finances its plan to regain full ownership of an Irish plant.

articles.title: Banks to Join BMO-Led $1.8 Billion Debt Deal for Honeywell Spinoff
articles.content: Bank of Montreal’s investment banking unit is leading a $1.8 billion debt deal supporting the sale of Honeywell International Inc.’s productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp.

articles.title: Alleged shooter charged with attempted assassination of Trump
articles.content: Cole Allen appears in court as White House blames critics for demonising the president

articles.title: Stocks Hold Record in Countdown to Tech Earnings: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks held at record highs ahead of a raft of megacap earnings that will test whether April’s rally is sustainable, with traders also awaiting updates from major central banks on the economic fallout of the Iran war.

articles.title: Private Credit Boom Pushes Fund Finance Market Past $1 Trillion
articles.content: The fund finance market has surged past $1 trillion, according to a pair of new reports, as money managers borrow more to manage liquidity and bridge delayed exits.

articles.title: Sotheby's US Private Sales Head on $130M Mnuchin Auction
articles.content: Sotheby's Head of Private Sales Courtney Kremers discusses the sale of 'unique' art world figure Robert Mnuchin's $130 million collection, and the shift towards younger buyers in the marketplace. She talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifled on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Starboard Builds Stake in Flowserve, Push for Changes
articles.content: Activist investor Starboard Value has built a significant stake in Flowserve Corp. and is speaking to the industrial company about potential changes, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: S&P, Nasdaq Continue Record Run | Closing Bell
articles.content: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Rogers Makes 10,000 Employees Eligible for Buyouts to Cut Costs
articles.content: Rogers Communications Inc., Canada’s largest wireless company, is making about 10,000 employees eligible for voluntary buyouts as the telecommunications industry deals with major growth and debt challenges.

articles.title: American Airlines Sells $1.14 Billion in Bonds for 32 Planes
articles.content: American Airlines Group Inc. on Monday sold $1.14 billion of bonds financing a group of planes.

articles.title: Intel $6.5 Billion Bond Sale Signals Investor Hope in Turnaround
articles.content: Intel Corp. has been suffering from credit rating downgrades in recent years as its profits deteriorated, but on Monday bond investors signaled they are buying into the company’s turnaround story.

articles.title: LifeMD Bone Longevity Lead on Osteoporosis Treatment, Prevention
articles.content: Dr. Doug Lucas, LifeMD Orthopedic Surgeon and Bone Health & Longevity Clinical Lead, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss bone health for men and women, diagnosing and treating osteoporosis, how to prevent bone health issues from beginning and compounding, and more. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: The Stock Market’s 'Curious Exuberance' Despite the Iran War
articles.content: On today’s Big Take podcast, Asia equities reporter Winnie Hsu and markets reporter Ruth Carson tell host Sarah Holder why global markets are looking past the Iran war — and why that could change. Listen wherever you get your podcasts. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Restart of US-Iran Peace Talks
articles.content: Gold fell as efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran remained at an impasse, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.

articles.title: Emerging Stocks Reclaim Pre-War Peak on AI, Hormuz Deal Hopes
articles.content: Emerging-market stocks climbed Monday amid a renewed focus on artificial intelligence companies, even as the stalemate between the US and Iran on resuming peace negotiations left the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and pushed oil higher.

articles.title: Hut 8 Raises $3.25 Billion to Help Fund Google-Tied Data Center
articles.content: A Hut 8 Corp. project sold $3.25 billion of investment-grade bonds Monday to fund the construction of a data center tied to Alphabet Inc.’s Google, the latest in a string of borrowing by firms spearheading the artificial intelligence boom.

articles.title: OpenAI and Microsoft loosen ties in revised AI deal
articles.content: Companies redraw $135bn alliance as ChatGPT maker seeks greater independence to increase revenues
